purpose: Provide reusable proof, evidence and drop-in governance templates so future waves can emulate evidence-coupled maturity scoring.
name: Proof Synchronization & Emulation Template

proof_maturity_levels.name: Conceptual speculation
proof_maturity_levels.description: Idea exists but is not yet anchored to equations, data or source evidence.

proof_maturity_levels.name: Architecture reasoning
proof_maturity_levels.description: Consistent with system architecture and interface logic.

proof_maturity_levels.name: Physics-derived
proof_maturity_levels.description: Supported by first-principles calculations or engineering equations.

proof_maturity_levels.name: Vendor-correlated
proof_maturity_levels.description: Correlated with vendor maps, datasheets or supplier evidence.

proof_maturity_levels.name: Operationally validated
proof_maturity_levels.description: Supported by commissioning, test or operational data.

proof_maturity_levels.name: Closed-loop adaptive evidence
proof_maturity_levels.description: Validated by recurring operational feedback and recalibration.

proof_maturity_levels.name: Long-term operational proof
proof_maturity_levels.description: Supported by long-term production data and stable governance history.

wave_emulation_rules: Every new wave must state previous score, current score and assumed next score.
Every new wave must separate visual progress from evidence-supported progress.
Every new wave must list proof level L0-L7 for each major claim.
Every new wave must include at least one risk of overconfidence or dilution.
Every new wave must define a drop-in message for the next agent or next wave.
Every new wave must identify what evidence would upgrade its maturity score.
drop_in_message_template: Continue HBHS-EP from the current branch and wave. First read source/json/wave_completion_metrics.json, source/json/evidence_proof_wave16.json and docs/screen/index.html. For the next wave, report previous score, current score, evidence-corrected score and assumed next score. Separate visual maturity from evidence-supported maturity. Assign proof levels L0-L7 to all major claims. Update the SSoT JSON first, then generate the browser dashboard. Do not increase maturity only because new visuals were added.
